incorrects.description: Student selected 6: counted the number of marks instead of reading the scale values

params.description: Soccer bar highlights. Scale marks become more visible.

steps.text: You counted the marks. But this scale counts by 2s. Each mark shows 2 students, not 1. Look at the numbers on the axis.

incorrects.description: Student selected 10: miscounted or misread the scale intervals

params.description: Soccer bar highlights. Scale numbers emphasize.

steps.text: Almost. Look closely at where the Soccer bar ends. The scale goes by 2s. Count: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. Where does the bar stop?

incorrects.description: Student selected 24: doubled the correct answer, possibly counting each mark as 2 twice

params.description: Soccer bar highlights. The endpoint at 12 pulses.

steps.text: That's too high. The bar ends at the mark labeled 12. We read the number where the bar stops, not double it.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: A guideline draws from the end of the Soccer bar down to the axis, landing at 12.

steps.text: Let me show you. I look at where the Soccer bar ends. I trace down to the axis. It lands at 12. The answer is 12 students. When the scale counts by 2s, we still read the number where the bar stops.
